description_long: This candle features a cluster of tall, vertically aligned wax columns with pointed tops, resembling sharpened pencils or stylized flames. The body of the candle is a deep brown, with subtle variations in height among the individual columns, giving it a rugged, handcrafted appearance. The base is broad and flat, transitioning into a sloped, greenish platform that appears to support the candle and catch any drips. The surface of the candle is matte and slightly rough, with no visible flame or wick present. There are no decorative holders or embellishments, and the design is minimalist yet striking, suitable for outdoor or rustic urban settings. The overall form suggests it is intended as a decorative piece rather than for practical illumination.
description: A cluster of tall, pointed brown wax columns on a broad, sloped greenish base, with a rugged, handcrafted look and no visible flame or wick.
description_view_0: Side view showing the tall, pointed candle columns and the sloped greenish base extending backward.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the jagged, uneven tops of the candle columns and the wide, flat base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with the sloped base and pointed candle tops visible.
description_view_3: Top-down view focusing on the arrangement and varying heights of the pointed candle columns.
